channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.eurogamer.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.eurogamer.net

item.title: Legendary designer Warren Spector's stealthy Thick of Thieves ditches PvP for solo and co-op as May release revealed
item.content:encoded:
Slipping onto PC, PlayStaton, and Xbox.

Perhaps you have fond memories of Deus Ex designer Warren Spector's legendary stealth game Thief: A Dark Project, or perhaps your sneaker of choice is Arkane Studios' more recent immersive sim Dishonored. Either way, you might be intrigued to hear Spector's latest effort, Thick as Thieves - which has been reimagined as solo and co-op game following its original PvPvE reveal - is launching on 20th May.

Announced back in 2024, Thick of Thieves unfolds in an alternate-history 1910s Scottish metropolis known as Kilcairn. It's a city of rain-slicked streets and soot-stained rooftops "steeped in intrigue and rich in opportunity"; where technology and magic combine, where portly guards patrol the night, and where imaginative ne'er-do-wells can do all sorts of looting with a bit of a plan.

Thick of Thieves was, you might recall, originally conceived as a game of immersive-sim-inspired competitive infiltration, which is the version I saw at the tail-end of 2024. But it turns out Spector's studio OtherSide Entertainment has shifted away from that initial vision as work has continued, and it's now focused on single-player and two-player co-op sneakery.

"As development progressed and the world of Kilcairn has come to life," it explained on Steam earlier this week, "we found that we were having more fun with solo and co-op play". So if all that initial talk of PvPvE was putting you off a little, then your interest might now be re-enflamed. It sounds like the mission-based core is still in tact though, with Thick of Thieves being explicitly designed for short play sessions.

Even I, the PvP-avoidant sort I usually am, was intrigued by Thick of Thieves in its earlier guise, so I'm curious to see how it plays now, given its change in direction. And with its 20th May release on PC, PlayStation 5, and Xbox Series X/S rapidly approaching, all will be revealed soon.

Related topics


https://www.eurogamer.net/thick-of-thieves-ditches-pvp-for-solo-and-co-op-as-may-release-revealed
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Serene climbing hit Cairn gets back On the Trail with free DLC - and a playable Marco - this summer
item.content:encoded:
Just when you thought you might get a moment to give your arms a rest, developer The Game Bakers has unveiled On the Trail, a series of free DLC releases for climbing hit Cairn that's set to begin this "summer".

Cairn, as a reminder, follows the adventures of professional climber Aava as she begins her ascent of the perilous Mount Kami. It's journey that takes her across a beautiful near-future landscape of meticulously framed vistas and dizzyingly vertiginous drops - all providing a soothing backdrop for what is, in reality, a surprisingly challenging game.

Progress unfolds across a series of plateaus, each presenting a fresh rock face to parse, as you hunt for a hopefully navigable route hidden among the mountain's many ledges and crevices. And then you'll begin your careful ascent, inching up one limb at a time - which is where my impatience rapidly became my undoing. But if you're of a more measured disposition, you'll likely be delighted to hear more Cairn is now on the way.

The Game Bakers has announced a series of free DLC that's arriving under the banner 'On the Trail'. Things get underway this "summer" with the first release, Deep Water, promising three new climbing locations and "fresh challenges" as players follow in the, uh, arm steps of a legendary climber.

As its name suggests, Dark Water an aquatically minded update, specifically introducing water soloing. What's that, you say? Well, as someone who's never climbed anything more exciting than a staircase in real-life, you're speaking to the wrong person, but The Game Bakers says players "can now climb over water, without the consequences of plummeting to their death". Oh, and Aava's agent Marco is being added as a playable character too.

So that's Cairn's free On the Trail DLC then, arriving - in part at least - this summer. And for another perspective on The Game Bakers' mighty climber, Eurogamer contributor Lewis Gordon had some thoughts about its "kinder, more generous" take on masocore earlier this year.

Related topics


https://www.eurogamer.net/climbing-hit-cairn-on-the-trail-free-dlc-this-summer
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Crimson Desert now has a detailed female character creator mod so you no longer have to play as "Temu Jon Snow"
item.content:encoded:
Divisive yet successful open-world fantasy game Crimson Desert notably does not come with a character creator. The game casts you as male hero Kliff and that's mostly who you are, besides some shorter stints as the characters Damiane and Oongka. You can change Kliff's hair and beard and tattoos later in the game, but you can't change his appearance structurally, or his gender. Until now, that is.

What the game lacks the modding community provides, and the "Character Creator - Female" mod on Nexus Mods adds exactly what it suggests: a female playable character with lots of customisation options. Specifically, that's 98 different face presets and 159 different hairstyles, which is more hairstyles than I believed existed in the real world. And they're accessed - once the mod is installed on PC - by visiting the barber in your Greymane settlement, once you've unlocked it.

Armour has been adjusted to better fit a feminine frame as well, and you can pair the mod with the Kliff Female Voice to complete the new look. What's more, future plans for the mod include adding male character customisation and additional player races such as orc and goblin, which sounds fun.

This isn't the first Crimson Desert mod that's allowed us to play persistently as a female character, mind you. As you may or may not know, there are sections in Crimson Desert where we play as the female character Damiane, and modders have used this as the basis for swapping us from Kliff to Damiane more permanently. Beware, though, there are not-safe-for-work variants of this out there, because of course there are.

The decision not to include character customisation in Crimson Desert was always strange when compared to Pearl Abyss's other big game, the MMO Black Desert Online, which was famed back in 2016 for its impressive and attractive character creation tools. It's a decision that's left many fans scratching their heads about why it was done. "Would have been 100 percent more interesting with a character creator," writes one of these fans, Tom VinPrice, in a Reddit thread about the female character creator mod. "You're right that it's baffling they didn't include one, but so were a lot of other design choices they've since fixed.

"Hopefully they at least add the ability to create your own version of Kliff or something, similar to how customizable male Shepard can be in Mass Effect. Crazy they really thought that after making probably one of the best character creators ever in their previous game that they then had the idea to force us all to play as Temu Jon Snow in their next game."

Crimson Desert was released at the end of March amidst huge expectations, but struggled to convince everyone. "A vast world and even vaster array of MMO-like activities mix with glittering fidelity in Crimson Desert, but what good is it without much character, texture or charm?" we wondered in our three-star Crimson Desert review. Nevertheless, Crimson Desert has sold well, notching up 4m sales as of 19th March, and the figure three weeks later ought to be notably higher.

If you're playing Crimson Desert, don't forget that we have guides to help you along. Of particular help are the Ancient Ruins and Desert Strongboxes guides, because they point out where in the vast open-world they are, and how to open or solve them.

Related topics


https://www.eurogamer.net/crimson-desert-female-player-character-customisation-mod
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
